title: Subleading Asymptotics of ECH Capacities and Symplectic Packing Problems
speaker: Dan Cristofaro-Gardiner
abstract: We compute the subleading asymptotics of the ECH and elementary ECH capacities of toric domains and show that they recover the perimeter in the liminf, without any genericity required.  As an application, we give the first examples of the failure of packing stability for compact manifolds with smooth boundary or with no boundary at all, addressing a question of Schlenk.  There are implications for long-term super-recurrence and ellipsoid embedding functions and if time allows I will also discuss a connection with symplectic tiling.   This will report on joint works with Hind, Magill, and McDuff.

proof_stages.label: ECH capacities, Weyl law, and discrepancy questions
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls ECH capacities as monotone symplectic size measurements, states their leading Weyl law, defines the subleading discrepancy e_k=c_k-sqrt(2k vol), and poses the main questions about boundedness, limits, and eventual sign.

proof_stages.label: Toric two-term asymptotic formula and affine perimeter
proof_stages.summary: For generalized convex toric domains X_Omega, the speaker states the joint theorem with Magill that liminf e_k equals minus one half of the SL(2,Z)-invariant affine perimeter of Omega, then explains the perimeter and its relation to a two-term Weyl law.

proof_stages.label: Perimeter obstruction for full fillings
proof_stages.summary: Using the two-term asymptotics together with capacity monotonicity, the talk states a full-filling obstruction: if a disjoint union of toric domains fully fills another, then the sum of the source affine perimeters must be at least the target affine perimeter.

proof_stages.label: Failure of Schlenk stable packing for identical domains
proof_stages.summary: After defining the k-packing number by identical rescaled copies, the perimeter obstruction is applied to answer Schlenk’s question negatively in dimension four by choosing domains with zero affine perimeter, with comments on the ball and CP2 targets and elementary ECH capacities.

proof_stages.label: Asymptotic packing, tiling, and systolic-ratio obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The final part weakens stable packing to the question of whether packing densities tend to one, relates this to symplectic tilings of R4, states a work-in-progress obstruction when the systolic ratio is at least two, and shows that asymptotic tiling would force all ECH discrepancies to be nonpositive.
